lines: India versus Pakistan. Quality of life only. Who comes out ahead?
Life expectancy favors India. Roughly 72 years versus 67 point 8.
Internet access also leans India, which changes education, work, and services.
GDP per person still favors India, though neither country is rich by global standards.
Infant mortality and health access remain major development challenges for both sides.
Literacy and school access are closer, but India still has the larger system and more capacity.
Pakistan faces similar pressures with fewer resources and a smaller economy.
So the overall quality-of-life picture is mixed, but India has the stronger baseline by the numbers.
Neither country is perfect. But on this scoreboard, India still leads.

seo.title: India vs Pakistan — Quality of Life Battle 📊
seo.description:
India vs Pakistan quality of life comparison: life expectancy, internet access, GDP per capita, literacy, and core living indicators.
